text.title: test-self-healing-locators-audited Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for test-self-healing-locators-audited. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The healer MUST extract the original locator's accessibility role AND accessible name (case-insensitive, whitespace-normalized regex). A heal candidate is acceptable ONLY when its role equals the original role AND its accessible name matches the original name pattern. Heals based on tag, CSS class, XPath structural index, or text-only matching are forbidden and MUST be rejected before being written to the diff.
rule.rationale: Role+name = "what the user thinks the element is"; class/structure = "how the framework rendered it last week." Drift in the latter is ubiquitous and harmless; drift in the former is a real change that should fail the test, not be auto-healed.

rule.statement: When the healer accepts a candidate, it MUST write the change as a unified diff to `healed-selectors.diff` (committed to the repo) and EXIT NONZERO so the CI run fails. A separate `healer-apply` job MUST refuse to run unless `healed-selectors.diff` carries an approving review marker (e.g., commit message tag `healer:approved` or a YAML side-car). High-stakes flow files (payments, auth) listed in `healer-policy.json` MUST be excluded from auto-heal entirely.
rule.rationale: Without the diff gate, a wrong heal becomes the new ground truth on the next CI run; with the gate, every heal is a code-review event the team sees and can reject.
